summary: Rich, full‑bodied audience recording that captures an energetic ’85 show with standout Terrapin Station and blazing Johnny B. Goode performances
review: The Oade brothers’ audience tape is warm, well balanced and surprisingly clear – the bass is tight, the crowd ambience vivid, though a few listeners note digi‑noise and occasional vocal fade. Musically the concert is high‑energy (rain/snow), featuring a superb Terrapin Station jam, a record‑fast Johnny B. Goode, and one of the best Supplication Jams of ’85, with solid playing from the whole band.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (mostly 5‑star) praise both the recording fidelity and the performance; only a handful of minor complaints keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Warm, full‑bodied sound with clear bass and good mix balance; occasional digi‑noise and a brief vocal fade after Space noted by some listeners.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set opened by rain/snow, strong interplay throughout; a few transitions (e.g., Man Smart) felt awkward but overall the pacing stayed lively and engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals praised as powerful despite occasional “froggy” tone; guitar work solid with blazing fast Johnny B. Goode, though he fades briefly after Space.
band_member_comments.Phil: Prominent, tight bass lines noted throughout; stepped in to help Jerry during the show, delivering strong supportive grooves.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Hammond work highlighted as exceptional on Supplication Jam, Terrapin Station and Man Smart; his solos described as rolling licks and even string‑like textures.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally solid rhythm section; a brief comment about drums being a limiting factor early in the show.
